Title: App Icon, Three Style Routes: Flat, Gradient, Glass
Teaser: Turns one app idea into three ship-ready app-icon prompts (flat, gradient, frosted glass) with named palettes, corner-radius framing and MJ params.
PromptHint: Name the [APP] and what it does, the [VIBE] (e.g. calm finance, bold fitness), and an optional [COLOR OR AVOID].
Prompt: You are a senior product designer who writes image-model prompts for app icons. App: [APP] - what it does. Desired vibe: [VIBE]. Color steer or things to avoid: [COLOR OR AVOID]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): the single glyph or shape that reads instantly at 60px, and why; reject tired metaphors (generic gear, plain chat bubble, lightbulb) unless truly apt. Produce THREE distinct icon routes for the SAME concept: (A) Flat, solid shapes, no shadow; (B) Gradient, smooth two-stop color, soft inner light; (C) Glass, frosted translucency, subtle refraction and a soft specular highlight. For EACH route write ONE copy-paste prompt that specifies: single centered glyph, bold simple silhouette, rounded-square iOS app icon framing with safe margin, a named 2-3 color palette WITH hex codes, even balance, flat solid background filling the tile, no text, no letters, no border, no photorealism, no clutter. OUTPUT FORMAT (Markdown table, columns: Route | Prompt | Midjourney params | Why it reads at small size). Use per route: '--ar 1:1 --style raw --no text, letters, photo, drop shadow, border'. After the table add one line: PICK - the strongest route for [VIBE] and why. Nothing else.

Title: Matched Icon Set: 8 Icons, One Visual Language
Teaser: Designs a cohesive 8-icon set from a feature list so every icon shares stroke weight, corner radius and grid - then writes a per-icon prompt plus one master style block.
PromptHint: List the [8 ITEMS] to iconify (e.g. home, search, profile, settings...), then set a [STYLE] (line, flat-fill, duotone) and [ACCENT COLOR].
Prompt: You are an icon-system designer who writes image-model prompts. Items to iconify: [8 ITEMS]. Style family: [STYLE] (line, flat-fill, or duotone). Accent color: [ACCENT COLOR]. THINK FIRST (silently): the shared rules that make a set feel like one family - stroke weight, corner radius, grid size, optical balance, level of detail; pick one metaphor per item that a stranger guesses correctly. FIRST OUTPUT a MASTER STYLE BLOCK: one reusable sentence of constraints (consistent stroke weight, 24px grid, rounded joins, generous padding, centered, flat background, no text, single color or stated duotone) to prepend to every icon. THEN OUTPUT a table (columns: Icon | Full prompt = master block + this glyph | One-line metaphor note) for all 8 items. Every glyph must be a clean single symbol, instantly legible, no labels, no extra props. Add a Midjourney line usable for the whole set: '--ar 1:1 --style raw --no text, gradient, photo, shadow'. End with: CONSISTENCY CHECK - 3 things to keep identical across all 8. Nothing else.

Title: Die-Cut Sticker Pack: 6 Playful Designs With White Border
Teaser: Generates a 6-piece cohesive sticker pack around a theme - bold outlines, thick white die-cut border, glossy finish - each as a copy-paste prompt on a transparent-ready background.
PromptHint: Give the [THEME] (e.g. cozy coffee, gym motivation, cats), the [TONE] (cute, retro, edgy), and an optional [PALETTE].
Prompt: You are a sticker-pack illustrator who writes image-model prompts. Theme: [THEME]. Tone: [TONE]. Palette steer: [PALETTE]. THINK FIRST (silently): 6 distinct but related subjects that feel like one collectible pack, and the recurring motif (a shared color, a wink, a prop) that ties them together. For EACH of the 6 write ONE copy-paste prompt specifying: single die-cut sticker, bold clean outline, thick white sticker border around the silhouette, flat vibrant cel-shaded fill, subtle glossy highlight, slight drop shadow under the cut edge, plain solid background for easy cutout, kawaii-friendly proportions if [TONE] suits, no scene, no text unless one short word is requested. OUTPUT FORMAT (numbered list 1-6: Subject - then the full prompt). After the list add: PACK GLUE - the one motif repeated across all 6. Add a Midjourney line: '--ar 1:1 --style raw --no busy background, text, realistic, scene'. Nothing else.

Title: Achievement Badge Set: Bronze, Silver, Gold Tiers
Teaser: Builds a gamification badge with three earned tiers that share a frame but escalate in richness - ribbons, gems, metallic shading - each tier a ready prompt.
PromptHint: Name the [ACHIEVEMENT] (e.g. 7-day streak, top contributor), the central [SYMBOL], and an optional [BRAND COLOR].
Prompt: You are a game-UI artist who writes image-model prompts for achievement badges. Achievement: [ACHIEVEMENT]. Central symbol: [SYMBOL]. Brand color: [BRAND COLOR]. THINK FIRST (silently): the shared badge silhouette (shield, circle medallion, hexagon) and how to make bronze, silver and gold feel earned - escalating metal tone, ribbon count, gem accents, glow - without changing the core shape. Produce THREE prompts, one per tier. EACH specifies: centered emblem badge, the [SYMBOL] at the heart, metallic rim with bevel and soft inner shading, small ribbon or laurel, tier-appropriate metal (warm bronze / cool silver / radiant gold), a subtle glow on gold only, flat solid background, crisp edges, no text, no numbers, no realistic photo. OUTPUT FORMAT (table: Tier | Prompt | What escalates vs the tier below). Add a Midjourney line for all three: '--ar 1:1 --style raw --no text, numbers, photo, flat lighting'. End with: LOCKED STATE - one prompt variant for the not-yet-earned greyed-out badge. Nothing else.

Title: Glassmorphism UI Icon On A Soft Gradient Tile
Teaser: Writes a premium frosted-glass icon prompt - translucent layers, blur, light refraction and a glossy edge highlight - sitting on a dreamy gradient backdrop.
PromptHint: Name the [GLYPH] (e.g. camera, wallet, weather sun), the [GRADIENT MOOD] (e.g. sunset peach, cool ocean), and an optional [ACCENT].
Prompt: You are a UI visual designer specializing in glassmorphism who writes image-model prompts. Glyph: [GLYPH]. Gradient mood: [GRADIENT MOOD]. Accent: [ACCENT]. THINK FIRST (silently): which parts of the glyph are frosted vs clear, where the single specular highlight lands, and how the background gradient glows through the glass. Then write ONE copy-paste prompt specifying: single centered [GLYPH] rendered as a frosted translucent glass object, soft inner blur, layered semi-transparent panels, crisp glossy edge highlight along the top-left, gentle colored light refraction in [ACCENT], floating on a smooth multi-stop gradient tile in [GRADIENT MOOD] with soft ambient glow, rounded-square framing, generous padding, clean and minimal, no text, no harsh shadow, no skeuomorphic clutter, no photoreal hands. OUTPUT FORMAT: print (1) the prompt as one paragraph, then (2) a Midjourney variant: '--ar 1:1 --style raw --no text, photo, hard shadow, noise'. Then add: VARIANT - one line swapping the gradient to a dark frosted-glass-on-black look. Nothing else.

Title: Emoji-Style Reaction Set: 6 Expressive Faces
Teaser: Creates a custom emoji-style reaction set - glossy 3D-ish faces with consistent geometry and clear emotions - as six copy-paste prompts ready for a chat or community.
PromptHint: Set the [CHARACTER BASE] (e.g. round yellow blob, your mascot, a cat), and list up to [6 EMOTIONS] (e.g. love, laugh, shocked, sad...).
Prompt: You are a custom-emoji designer who writes image-model prompts. Character base: [CHARACTER BASE]. Emotions: [6 EMOTIONS]. THINK FIRST (silently): the fixed face geometry (eye spacing, mouth zone, head shape) that must stay identical, then which 2-3 features move per emotion so each reads instantly at thumbnail size. FIRST OUTPUT a BASE BLOCK: one reusable sentence locking the look (same [CHARACTER BASE], glossy soft-3D finish, smooth rounded forms, simple expressive features, soft top light with a single highlight, centered, flat solid background, no text). THEN a table (columns: Emotion | Full prompt = base block + this expression | The 2-3 features that change). Keep all 6 visually a family - same proportions, same shading. Add a Midjourney line: '--ar 1:1 --style raw --no text, realistic human, scene, busy background'. End with: SET TEST - look away, glance back; if any emotion is ambiguous, name the fix. Nothing else.

Title: Line-Style Outline Icon Pack For A Clean UI
Teaser: Produces a uniform stroke-icon pack - even line weight, rounded caps, pixel-grid alignment - as one master block plus a per-icon table, the safe default for product UIs.
PromptHint: List the [ICONS NEEDED] (e.g. upload, download, share, edit, delete...) and set the [STROKE FEEL] (e.g. thin elegant, bold friendly).
Prompt: You are a UI icon designer who writes image-model prompts for outline icons. Icons needed: [ICONS NEEDED]. Stroke feel: [STROKE FEEL]. THINK FIRST (silently): the exact line language - stroke weight, rounded vs square caps, corner radius, grid (e.g. 24px), and the optical-balance rule so a circle icon doesn't look smaller than a square one. FIRST OUTPUT a MASTER BLOCK: one reusable sentence (single-color outline icon, consistent [STROKE FEEL] stroke weight, rounded caps and joins, aligned to a 24px grid, centered with generous padding, no fill, flat white or transparent-ready background, no text, no shadow). THEN a table (columns: Icon | Full prompt = master block + this glyph | Metaphor in 4 words). Every icon must be one clean symbol, monoline, unmistakable. Add a Midjourney line: '--ar 1:1 --style raw --no fill, gradient, text, photo, shadow'. End with: PAIR-PROOFING - which two icons risk looking alike, and how to differentiate them. Nothing else.

Title: Soft 3D Clay Icon With Gradient And Bounce
Teaser: Crafts a tactile soft-3D clay-render icon prompt - rounded extruded shapes, matte finish, gentle gradient lighting - the trendy chunky look used across modern app stores.
PromptHint: Name the [OBJECT] (e.g. rocket, lock, headphones), the [COLOR THEME] (e.g. coral and cream), and an optional [MATERIAL TWIST] (e.g. glossy, matte clay).
Prompt: You are a 3D icon artist who writes image-model prompts for soft-render app icons. Object: [OBJECT]. Color theme: [COLOR THEME]. Material twist: [MATERIAL TWIST]. THINK FIRST (silently): which forms to round and extrude for a chunky friendly read, where the soft key light sits, and the single accent that pops. Then write ONE copy-paste prompt specifying: single centered [OBJECT] as a soft 3D render, rounded extruded clay-like forms, smooth matte surface (or [MATERIAL TWIST]), gentle gradient in [COLOR THEME], soft ambient occlusion and a single big soft highlight, subtle floor contact shadow, playful chunky proportions, rounded-square icon framing with padding, plain solid or soft-gradient background, no text, no busy detail, no realistic photo, no human. OUTPUT FORMAT: print (1) the prompt as one paragraph, then (2) a Midjourney variant: '--ar 1:1 --style raw --no text, photo, sharp edges, clutter'. Add: SCALE NOTE - one tweak so it still reads at 48px. Nothing else.

Title: Loyalty And Status Badges: Member Tier Crests
Teaser: Designs a tier of membership crest badges (e.g. Member to Founder) that share a frame and escalate prestige - clean flat-vector crests with no text, ready to drop into a profile.
PromptHint: List the [TIERS] (e.g. Member, Pro, VIP, Founder), the [BRAND SYMBOL or initial], and an optional [PALETTE].
Prompt: You are a brand badge designer who writes image-model prompts for status crests. Tiers: [TIERS]. Brand symbol or initial: [BRAND SYMBOL]. Palette: [PALETTE]. THINK FIRST (silently): the shared crest silhouette and how prestige climbs across tiers - more facets, richer color, a subtle glow, added laurel - while [BRAND SYMBOL] stays centered and recognizable. Produce ONE prompt per tier. EACH specifies: centered flat-vector crest badge, [BRAND SYMBOL] at the core, clean geometric construction, defined 2-3 color palette WITH hex from [PALETTE], crisp edges, subtle tier-escalating accent (top tier gets a soft inner glow), flat solid background, no photorealism, no 3D bevel unless requested, no text, no numbers. OUTPUT FORMAT (table: Tier | Prompt | What signals higher status). Add a shared Midjourney line: '--ar 1:1 --style raw --no text, numbers, photo, gradient noise'. End with: RANK READ - one line confirming a stranger could order the tiers by sight alone. Nothing else.

Title: Functional UI Elements: Buttons, Toggles, Cards
Teaser: Generates clean prompts for reusable interface components - a primary button, a toggle switch, a stat card - in a chosen style, isolated on a neutral canvas for compositing.
PromptHint: Pick the [COMPONENTS] (e.g. primary button, toggle, slider, card), the [STYLE] (flat, neumorphic, glass), and an [ACCENT COLOR].
Prompt: You are a UI designer who writes image-model prompts for interface components. Components: [COMPONENTS]. Style: [STYLE] (flat, neumorphic, or glass). Accent color: [ACCENT COLOR]. THINK FIRST (silently): the shared design tokens - corner radius, shadow depth, padding rhythm, accent usage - that make these components feel like one kit, and how the chosen [STYLE] expresses each. FIRST OUTPUT a KIT BLOCK: one reusable sentence (consistent corner radius, [STYLE] surface treatment, [ACCENT COLOR] used for active state, generous padding, centered single component, soft neutral background, no real text - use simple placeholder blocks for labels, no clutter, no device frame). THEN a table (columns: Component | Full prompt = kit block + this element | The interactive state shown, e.g. default / active). Keep depth, radius and accent identical across all. Add a Midjourney line: '--ar 4:3 --style raw --no real text, device frame, photo, busy background'. End with: KIT GLUE - 3 tokens to keep constant. Nothing else.

Title: Animated-App-Store Icon Concept: 3 Variations To A-B Test
Teaser: Produces three competing app-icon directions for the same product so you can A-B test - distinct concepts, not recolors - each a full prompt with a rationale.
PromptHint: Describe the [APP] and category, the [AUDIENCE] (e.g. busy parents, gamers), and an optional [MUST-INCLUDE element].
Prompt: You are a growth-minded app designer who writes image-model prompts for store icons. App: [APP] and category. Audience: [AUDIENCE]. Must-include: [MUST-INCLUDE]. THINK FIRST (silently): what makes an icon win in a crowded search grid for [AUDIENCE] - bold silhouette, high contrast, one idea, color that pops against neighbors; then invent THREE genuinely different concepts (different metaphor or composition), not three recolors. For EACH concept write ONE copy-paste prompt specifying: single centered focal element, bold high-contrast silhouette readable at 60px, rounded-square iOS framing with safe margin, a named 2-3 color palette WITH hex, flat solid background, [MUST-INCLUDE] if given, no text, no fine detail, no photoreal, no border. OUTPUT FORMAT (table: Concept name | Prompt | Why it could win in search | Risk). Add per concept: '--ar 1:1 --style raw --no text, fine detail, photo, border'. End with: TEST PLAN - one line on which concept to ship first and what to measure. Nothing else.

Title: Duotone Icon Pack: Two-Color Depth Without Gradients
Teaser: Builds a stylish duotone icon set where a solid base plus a translucent accent layer creates depth - one master block plus a per-icon table, perfect for modern marketing pages.
PromptHint: List the [ICONS] (e.g. speed, security, sync, support...), the [BASE COLOR] and the [ACCENT COLOR].
Prompt: You are an icon designer who writes image-model prompts for duotone icons. Icons: [ICONS]. Base color: [BASE COLOR]. Accent color: [ACCENT COLOR]. THINK FIRST (silently): the duotone rule - solid base shape plus one semi-transparent accent shape layered to imply depth, applied identically to every icon so the set looks intentional, not random. FIRST OUTPUT a MASTER BLOCK: one reusable sentence (flat duotone icon, solid [BASE COLOR] primary shape with a single semi-transparent [ACCENT COLOR] overlay for depth, consistent geometric construction, rounded joins, centered with padding, flat white or transparent-ready background, no gradient, no text, no shadow). THEN a table (columns: Icon | Full prompt = master block + this glyph | Where the accent layer sits). Keep the two-color logic identical everywhere. Add a Midjourney line: '--ar 1:1 --style raw --no gradient, text, photo, third color'. End with: COLOR CHECK - one line confirming the accent stays a tint, never a second random hue. Nothing else.

Title: Notification And Status Micro-Icons: Tiny But Legible
Teaser: Designs the small system glyphs - success, warning, error, info, new - as a tight family that stays crisp at 16px, with consistent shape coding and a color rationale.
PromptHint: List the [STATES] (e.g. success, warning, error, info, new) and set the [SHAPE LANGUAGE] (e.g. circles, rounded squares) plus optional [BRAND ACCENT].
Prompt: You are a systems icon designer who writes image-model prompts for tiny status glyphs. States: [STATES]. Shape language: [SHAPE LANGUAGE]. Brand accent: [BRAND ACCENT]. THINK FIRST (silently): the smallest set of distinct shapes that survive at 16px (check, exclamation, x, i, dot), the universal color coding (green/amber/red/blue/accent) and how to keep them one family while each stays unmistakable. FIRST OUTPUT a MASTER BLOCK: one reusable sentence (single tiny status icon inside a [SHAPE LANGUAGE] container, bold simple inner glyph, high contrast, one solid color per state, centered, flat background, designed to stay legible at 16px, no text, no gradient, no fine detail). THEN a table (columns: State | Full prompt = master block + this glyph + its color | Why it reads at 16px). Add a Midjourney line: '--ar 1:1 --style raw --no fine detail, text, gradient, photo'. End with: SHRINK TEST - one tweak per icon if any blurs when scaled down. Nothing else.
